findings.description: The mandatory contrast-testing requirement (test every text/background pair against WCAG AA minimums of 4.5:1 body / 3:1 large text, using webaim.com or equivalent) has no corresponding rule at all — it is dropped entirely from the candidate set.

payload.description: Test contrast levels for every text/background pair using webaim.com or equivalent; meet WCAG AA minimums (4.5:1 body text, 3:1 large text >=24px or 19px bold).

findings.description: The 'don't rely on color alone' accessibility directive (pair with icons/depth/copy/feedback) is a distinct, separately governed rule and is missing from the candidate rules entirely.

payload.description: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; pair with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

findings.description: Rule conflates two distinct directives with opposing polarity: a positive 'known-safe pairs' recommendation (must/allowed) and a negative 'avoid gold/sunshine text on white' constraint (must_not). Single must_not polarity misrepresents the affirmative safe-pairs guidance.

new_rules.description: Use known-safe color pairs: Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy/Graphite on White/Light Blue/Golden Sand; White on Brand Blue/Deep Blue/Dark Navy.
Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White (fails contrast); golds are reserved for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

findings.description: Existing rule omits the governance/scope exception that gold/sunshine are permitted for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis — losing the qualifying condition attached to the must_not directive.

governance.exception_scope: golds permitted for surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only

findings.description: Applying a single hard_constraint/must_not classification across the whole merged rule mischaracterizes the safe-pairs portion, which is affirmative/recommended usage rather than a prohibition.
